message: Last weekend I burned—and I mean *really* burned—our Sunday morning toast. Smoke everywhere, alarm screaming, kids laughing like it was a fire drill comedy show. And honestly? I rolled with it. We ended up opening all the windows, doing a kitchen dance party while the air cleared, and I actually tested whether everyone knew where to meet outside (they did, because we'd made it a game months ago). Turned a minor kitchen disaster into a cozy little family check-in. No stress, just… real. Turns out the best time to think about home fire safety isn't in a panic—it's on a regular Sunday when you can laugh about it together. That little refresher really helped me feel like we've actually got this. Do you ever sneak in safety stuff with your family without making it feel like a chore?
